content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON
CONNELLY
HAGEL
FONG &
LEIDIGH

[illegible]
JUN 2 9 25 AM*

June 1, 1992


Ms. Linda Cassidy
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

RE: CONFIRMATION OF TELEPHONE ADVICE REGARDING
TRANSMITTAL OF LATE CONTRIBUTION/LATE INDEPENDENT
EXPENDITURE REPORTS

Dear Ms. Cassidy:

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE WHITES
DIANE M. FISHBURN

Pursuant to our recent telephone conversations,
this letter seeks written confirmation (formal written
advice) regarding the appropriate methods for
transmission of late Contribution Reports and late
Independent Expenditure Reports. This advice is sought
on behalf of Lance H. Olson, who is treasurer for
numerous committees that are subject to these reporting
requirements.

I posed two questions to you. I am seeking
written confirmation of your responses because neither
you nor I could locate specific prior written advice
from the Commission which confirms the advice you gave
me. In the future, the response to this letter should
do that.

Question 1: When a contribution is received by a
committee on day one and the contribution is subject to
late contribution reporting, is it permissible to
deposit the late Contribution Report in the guaranteed
overnight delivery pickup within 24 hours of receipt
(i.e., on day two)? This question arose because some
clients of ours had received conflicting advice
indicating that the Late Contribution Report had to be
filed at one (at the filing officer's location) within
24 hours of receipt.

Answer 1: The Late Contribution Report complies
with the requirements of Section 84203(b) if it is
deposited with an appropriate guaranteed overnight

(Of Counsel)
LLOYD G. CONNELLY

CapitolBank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Telephone (916) 442-2952
Facsimile (916) 442-1280

Ms. Linda Cassidy
June 1, 1992
Page 2

delivery service within 24 hours of receipt (i.e., the next
calendar day).

     The same is true of a Late Independent Expenditure Report.
It meets the requirements of Section 84204(a) if it is deposited
with an appropriate guaranteed overnight delivery service within
24 hours of the expenditure being made.

     For makers of late contributions, the deposit with an
appropriate guaranteed overnight delivery service must occur
within 24 hours of when the contribution was "made" as that term
is defined in Regulation 18421.1(b).

     Question 2:  For purposes of Late Contribution Reports and
Late Independent Expenditure Reports under Sections 84203 and
84204, does an appropriate guaranteed overnight delivery service
include commercial services which provide guaranteed overnight
delivery (such as Federal Express, UPS, DHL, Airborne, etc.)? Or
is it limited to "guaranteed overnight mail through the United
States Postal Service"?

     Answer 2:  Based upon the Bagatelos Advice Letter,
No. A-86-305, you advised that any commercial guaranteed
overnight delivery service may be utilized; it is not limited to
just the United States Postal Service. Effective with 1987, the
Political Reform Act has provided that the Supplemental Pre-
Election Statements may be filed using "guaranteed overnight
delivery service." (Section 84200.7(a)(2) and (b)(2); Section
8400.8(b) and (c).) The Commission staff has concluded that this
more inclusive provision best serves the purposes of the Act and
is interpreting the provisions of Sections 84203 and 84204 to
allow for transmission of these reports by any "guaranteed
overnight delivery service" which includes services other than
the United States Postal Service.

     Thank you for your attention to this matter. If you have
any questions, please do not hesitate to call me.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDICH

[signature]
ROBERT E. LEIDICH
REL:jlh
